title: Today's AI Brief: 100 things we announced at I/O 2026
summary: Google's I/O 2026 announcements, OpenAI's Codex with GPT-5.5, and AI updates

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to walk you through some of the top AI headlines. First up, Google's I/O 2026 conference was packed with announcements - a whopping 100 of them. I'm looking at the list, and it's clear that the company is making big bets on AI across the board. From new model releases to developer tools, Google is aiming to make AI more accessible and useful for everyone.

One of the standout announcements from I/O 2026 is the expansion of Google's AI capabilities. What stands out to me is the breadth of applications, from language models to computer vision. It's a lot to take in, but the gist is that Google is pushing the boundaries of what's possible with AI.

Next, I'm looking at how Ramp engineers are using Codex with GPT-5.5 to supercharge their code review process. Apparently, they're getting substantive feedback in minutes instead of hours. That's a game-changer for developers, who can now focus on writing code rather than reviewing it.

Also on the board is the ongoing conversation around AI safety and trust. As AI becomes more pervasive, it's clear that companies are prioritizing responsible AI development. My read is that this is an area where we'll see continued innovation and progress in the coming months.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
